DESIGN PHASE COMPLETE — 3 specs merged: ① Credit Ledger (prepaid-ZAR metering modelled on Anthropic's prepaid wallet, #106/#108); ④a Entitlement resolver + add-on layer (closes a live entitlement self-grant vuln, #107); ② askRoss Agent (tool-use agent, bands A+B = read + workflow/run ops, graduated-risk policy engine auto/confirm/off + kill switch + audit, Sonnet 4.6/streaming SSE/prompt caching, ledger+entitlement pre-flight gates, 20-prompt eval harness; §3.1 measurement-gates submitResponse so the agent never fabricates a compliance record, #110). Sequence: thin slice of both wheels → launch → then flip ③ Payment Rail live.

PR-1b rules + reflect — NEW-CRIT-01 closed, CRIT-11 mitigated — closed the cross-tenant WhatsApp routing hijack (whatsapp-numbers / location-whatsapp-mapping write cascade) and mitigated the self-approvable rewards node. The first rewards draft was silently defeated by the auth.token.phone_number null-equality pathology (no user in this app has that claim → null === null GRANTS on create) — caught by security review pre-deploy and logged as its own High bug row. #177 folded the session's 4 new security findings + the CRIT-09 escalation into the triage queue.

askRoss — snoozeCard removed from the agent registry — npm run eval -- --only=snooze --repeat=3 returned 0/3 with no tools called, and the finding inverted: Ross was right, the test was wrong. snoozeCard required a cardId that nothing in the agent's world supplies, so the only way to call it was to invent one — which the adapter sanitised and wrote as a real snooze. The 2026-06-07 eval had scored that fabrication as a PASS. Removed rather than re-grounded on two independent grounds: snooze is the only agent action that makes information *disappear* (works against Ross's own purpose, especially the W2 proactive sweep), and capability-absence beats prompt-guarding. User-facing snooze path (rossV2Snooze) untouched. Ready agent tools 9 → 8.

Eval harness ergonomics — surface the tools Ross actually invoked on a failing case (so a judgment failure can be told apart from a harness failure without re-reading transcripts) + --only / --repeat flags for isolating and repeat-running a single case. This is what made the #175 snoozeCard diagnosis possible.

PR-1a rules — scanningData, locations root, whatsapp-history, admin-claims LOCKED + LIVE-VERIFIED — first tranche of the OWASP RTDB rules batch, deployed and probed in prod: CRIT-04 (non-admin write 401 at root and $dataId), HIGH-04 (locations root .write → false, closed by construction), MED-06 (whatsapp-message-history write 401), LOW-03 (admin-claims non-admin read 401 / admin read 200). #172 made the probe script safe to run in the wrong order; #173 corrected two mislabelled locations checks and recorded the closures.

Live location-creation bug fixed (ownerId) — client-side location creation had been broken for every non-admin user since 2026-05-31 (PR #100): database.rules.json:49 requires ownerId === auth.uid on create, but location-service.js (the live Vue dashboard path) wrote userId/createdBy and no ownerId. Hidden for ~7 weeks because onboarding writes ownerId, admins bypass via the token clause, and the failure surfaced only as a generic toast with the real reason in console.error. Both client payloads now write ownerId: uid after the spread (a caller-supplied value can't override the authenticated uid), guarded by 5 vitest cases that fail RED against the pre-fix code. #169 re-censused the rules batch and logged the bug.

ROSS-FoodCost-v2 — design + Deliverable 1 (Ross food-cost reader) — getFoodCostSummary promoted pending→READY: a read-only, access-checked (C-1 caller→location gate, fail-closed cross-tenant test) adapter over the denormalised summary already persisted at locations/{loc}/stockUsage — no calculator — returning cost %, trend, and low-stock items. Brainstorm → 3-agent adversarial review → spec v2 → subagent-driven TDD across 4 tasks; 165/165 + build green; review folded (orderByKey().limitToLast(20) read bound, ESM→CJS test). Sequenced next: D2 calculator → "what to order"; D3 v2 UI + CSV mapping-memory; D4 strip-and-harden. #166 landed the OWASP RTDB rules-batch remediation plan in the same window.

OWASP scan consolidation (#168). Landed four orphaned scheduled scans (2026-07-02/07-04/07-05/07-19) that had committed findings to PR-less branches for up to 19 days. RETRACTED the false HIGH-08 closure (@grpc/grpc-js 1.12.6 is the last VULNERABLE version, not a patched one - advisory range >=1.12.0 <1.12.7; verified still open by live npm audit). Folded in the 2026-07-19 follow-up's 11 new findings (5 Critical incl. guests cross-tenant PII, receipts Storage exposure, forgeable userLocations, self-approvable rewards, Meraki secret in Cloud Logging). Reconciled a cross-scan duplicate (HIGH-09 = NEW-CRIT-01). Reconciled totals: 11 Criticals + 11 Highs open. Docs/data only.

CRIT-07 gen2 fix + LIVE-ENFORCED in prod (#164). The #162 monitor soak caught a real valid:false on prod traffic: gen2 Cloud Functions strip the function-name path (req.originalUrl='/'), so reconstruction yielded '.../' while Twilio signs the full '.../receiveWhatsAppMessage' -> HMAC mismatch. Fix: verifyTwilioSignature validates against an explicit TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL (env or comma/space list, valid-if-any; multi-URL accept is safe since forging needs the token), falling back to reconstruction only when unset. +5 tests reproducing the exact gen2 failure (22/22); review folded (TWILIO_WEBHOOK_URL in WHATSAPP_BOT_SOP.md, single error-log per call). Then deployed + verified live: monitor -> valid:true -> TWILIO_SIGNATURE_MODE=enforce -> real message processed under mode:'enforce'. CRIT-07 fully closed in prod with ZERO downtime across the whole rollout.

CRIT-07 — Twilio webhook signature verification (#162). Closes the forge-a-guest-message vector on the live WhatsApp webhook: new functions/utils/twilio-signature.js verifies X-Twilio-Signature (HMAC-SHA1) via the official twilio.validateRequest using TWILIO_TOKEN. Scoped to the only LIVE handler (receiveWhatsAppMessageEnhanced — live-caller trace found the non-enhanced file + whatsappApp express block are dead). Staged rollout via TWILIO_SIGNATURE_MODE (monitor default → enforce → off) so a URL-reconstruction mismatch can't dark-out a live channel. 17 tests exercise the REAL validator with crypto-generated signatures; local security+code review + automated review all clean (folded comma-list-host hardening + 2 guard tests + WHATSAPP_BOT_SOP docs; declined a pure-CJS test rewrite — mixed import/require is the repo convention). DEPLOY-GATED: operator runs the monitor soak then flips to enforce. Spun off a PII-log redaction task + dead-code cleanup suggestion.

OWASP 2026-06-15 audit findings logged (#161). 29 new findings (7 Critical · 8 High · 9 Medium · 5 Low) logged in the Bug Triage Queue with assigned models + remediation order; High-Priority item 4 + project-status security panel (dual-audit history). Rebased onto master (resolved the lastUpdatedNote/security-panel conflict, preserving #160's W2-deployed note + phase-8). Review-verified against live code: CRIT-06 severity overstated (public Meraki handshake echo, not a credential), plus overlaps flagged with the prior audit (CRIT-05≈H-2 Bucket C, MED-02 CSP, grpc/vite CVEs). ③ Payment Rail backend — Paystack credit top-ups (PAYG) + trial, DORMANT (#156). All 11 TDD tasks executed: functions/payments/ 6 modules + 4 onRequest CF shells (webhook signature-gated, no Bearer) + 2 committed-not-run seeds + index wiring + catalog (both copies). Webhook = sole grant source of truth: HMAC-SHA512 over the RAW body (constant-time), write-before-effect idempotency (RTDB transaction claim, abort-on-exists), grant ALWAYS re-derived server-side from the bundle. Security+code reviews pre-PR: all 8 design intents HOLD; hardening folded (405 + null-rawBody guards, key whitelist, currency/status assertions, generic 500s, reconciliation notes, webhook handler smoke tests). Fixed a plan defect: fake-rtdb transaction() abort semantics + guard test. 30 payments / 252 functions tests, build green. Pre-launch hardening → backlog 3d.

Spec (docs/plans/2026-06-07-payment-rail-paystack-topups-design.md): PAYG credit top-ups + free trial via Paystack (web-verified Stripe isn't SA-direct + Paystack-SA can't charge USD → USD unit-of-account/1:1 grant, ZAR per-bundle charge); USD bundles $20/$99/$200; webhook = sole source of truth (HMAC-SHA512, idempotent on reference, server-derived amount); rossAgent→Free (PAYG-for-all). Backend plan (docs/plans/2026-06-08-payment-rail-backend-plan.md): 11 TDD tasks for functions/payments/ (16 tests), onRequest+Bearer (reconciled from onCall), reuses ledger.grantCredit. askRoss eval → 21/21: owner-context literal due dates — buildOwnerContext now renders overdue + upcoming items with the actual due date (SA DD/MM/YYYY, reformatted from the digest's normalised ISO nextDueDate), fixing the q-overdue/q-today judge-flap (Ross derived 25/05/2026 = today − 11d, but the digest only said '11 days overdue' so the Haiku judge intermittently flagged the date as fabrication). Give the judge the literal value it checks. Also a real product win (owners get the date). Guard: rossChat.test.js asserts the formatted date appears. askRoss eval — execute.js db seam (Group B was a harness bug, not Ross) — the eval driver seamed rossChat/tools/ledger to the fake RTDB but NOT execute.js, whose own getDb() backs executeTool's audit-write + no-undo capture. So every tool's run() succeeded then threw 'Can't determine Firebase Database URL' at the audit step, and Ross honestly reported the error. The eval-tuning plan called this 'Group B: Ross won't call his tools / confabulates' — a misdiagnosis from reading Ross's prose instead of transcript.toolResults (which showed the real {error}). Fix: execute.__setDbForTests(db). Guard: the driver test now asserts the captured output isn't an {error} envelope. q-staff/q-staff-runs/snooze now pass with real tool data. Review: security-clear, safe to merge.

CF auth-posture guard tests (guard-backfill 3c seed #4) — source-scan vitest suites asserting the admin-only Cloud Functions are genuinely gated: requireAdmin (index.js) + verifyAdmin/verifySuperAdmin (entitlements/auth.js) each have dual-factor structure (custom claim AND RTDB record; either alone → 403), and 8 CFs (entitlementSetTier/GrantAddOn/CancelAddOn + getGoogleConfig + 4 queue mutators) call their guard. 20 tests, no security holes found. Source-scan shape because vi.mock('firebase-admin') can't intercept CJS require() in nested functions/node_modules. Secondary debris-denylist task dropped (entangled with intentional illustrative content). Review: 🟡 (verifyAuthToken count inflated by the require import → fixed to await verifyAuthToken( anchor, floor >=4) + 2 🔵 folded.

Phase 7 ② askRoss tool-schema dialect fix — Ask Ross was 400ing every turn. toAnthropicTools emitted draft-4 boolean exclusiveMinimum; Anthropic requires JSON Schema draft 2020-12. Dropped {target:openApi3} for the default jsonSchema7 (numeric exclusiveMinimum); the jsonSchema2020-12 target is itself buggy. Found via prod logs (139 server tests all mocked the LLM client). +2 regression guards, 175/175 green, redeployed + verified live.

Phase 7 ② askRoss slice 5 — Ask Ross ⌘K client: streams the rossChat SSE CF into a multi-turn command-palette modal with inline confirm-cards + terminal/error banners. 3 pure/thin modules (frame parser, immutable reducer, fetch()+ReadableStream transport with a FLAT body — EventSource cant POST/auth) + 3 components (no v-html). Retires the scripted askRoss() stub. 20 unit tests; build + 173/173 functions green. Subagent-driven, per-task + final review (2 folded, 1 declined). Desktop only (mobile→3a).

Phase 7 ② askRoss slice 4 — confirm-flow pause/resume + 4 confirm-tier tools. The agent now authors the playbook with the owner in the loop: a confirm-tier tool (activate/create/edit/pause workflow) PAUSES → persists a pending action at ross/agentPending/{uid}/{turnId} (10-min expiry, server-keyed, uid-scoped) + debits Request A (abandoned confirms still bill, §2.1) + emits a confirm card → the owner's resume ({resumeTurnId, decision}) re-runs the pre-flight gates (kill-switch holds), checks expiry (fail-closed), atomically one-time-consumes the pending (no double-fire), executes (audited confirmed:<uid>) or declines, then continues the loop (Request B, distinct requestId). HALF B: extracted rossActivateWorkflow/rossCreateWorkflow/rossUpdateWorkflow write-logic into owner-callable *AsOwner cores in ross.js shared by BOTH the CF handler and the agent adapters (byte-for-byte handler parity; coded errors → HTTP status or tool_result); promoted the 4 confirm tools pending→READY. bugs.title: Every RTDB rule written against auth.token.phone_number GRANTS instead of denying — no user in this app has that claim.
bugs.notes: There is no phone-based sign-in anywhere in the client (rg "signInWithPhoneNumber|RecaptchaVerifier|PhoneAuthProvider" public → zero hits; the only paths are createUserWithEmailAndPassword/signInWithEmailAndPassword), and every setCustomUserClaims call writes only {admin} and overwrites the whole claims object (functions/index.js:782,987,992,1321). So auth.token.phone_number is null for everyone — and RTDB evaluates null === null as TRUE, so any rule comparing it against a data.child(...) value that is also null (i.e. on a CREATE) grants rather than denies. This is not theoretical: it silently defeated the first draft of PR #176's rewards fix, caught by security review before deploy. Still to re-derive under this light: guest-rewards/$phoneNumber (database.rules.json:88) — its .write is admin || auth.token.phone_number === $phoneNumber, which is never true for a real user, so that node is effectively admin-only in practice (and is one reason receipt-management's reward flow is broken end-to-end, see the row below). Audit every remaining phone_number rule and replace null-mismatch reliance with explicit existence tests. Found 2026-07-21 via the PR #176 security review.

bugs.title: whatsapp-message-history root .read has the identical cascade pathology PR #176 fixes one node over
bugs.notes: database.rules.json:349 is ".read": "auth != null", which cascades and makes the per-child per-tenant rule at :354 dead code. Any authenticated user reads every tenant's {locationId, phoneNumber, content, …} — guest phone numbers and full message bodies, cross-tenant. POPIA-relevant. PR-1a locked this node's .write only (MED-06a); the read side was never in scope. Not a NEW-CRIT-01 routing bypass (the phoneNumber here is the *guest's*, not the routing number), so it was correctly left out of PR #176 — but it needs its own read census (direct-path vs root-query consumers) before the root can be locked. Found 2026-07-21 via the PR #176 security review.

bugs.title: receipt-management.js reward creation is rejected by rules and has been for a long time — the flow is broken END TO END.
bugs.notes: processRewards (public/js/receipt-management.js:952-964) writes a reward payload of {receiptId, campaignId, guestPhoneNumber, totalAmount, status, createdAt, createdBy}, but rewards/$rewardId .validate requires newData.hasChildren(['metadata','status','value','expiresAt']) — the payload has status only, and the hasChildren term is an unconditional AND that not even an admin bypasses. The two follow-on writes fail as well: campaign-rewards/{cid}/{rid} requires admin (database.rules.json:93,96) and guest-rewards/{phone}/{rid} requires auth.token.phone_number === $phoneNumber (:88), which per the row above is null for every user. Sibling reward-management.js:661 (manual reward issue) supplies all four keys and works — presumably why this went unnoticed. Fix: add the missing fields to the payload (mirror reward-management.js:661), or route reward creation through a CF. Not bundled into PR #176 — it is a client payload bug, not a rules bug. Third live bug a rules census has surfaced (after locations/ownerId and this), all three hidden behind a client catch that only console.errors. Found 2026-07-21 during the PR-1b census, independently confirmed by security review.

bugs.title: CRIT-09 IS WORSE THAN AUDITED, AND THE AUDITED FIX WOULD NOT WORK — every WhatsApp receipt image is world-readable on the public internet, no account required.
bugs.notes: functions/receiptProcessor.js:440 calls await file.makePublic() on every receipt image, then stores the URL as https://storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/receipts/{timestamp}_receipt.jpg (:421,:443). Three consequences: (1) storage.rules is IRRELEVANT for these objects — makePublic() writes an allUsers:READ ACL onto the GCS object, and requests to storage.googleapis.com are served by GCS directly and never evaluate Firebase Storage rules. So the audit's proposed remediation (tighten allow read on /receipts/{receiptFile}) would neither break anything nor fix anything — a false closure waiting to happen, exactly the HIGH-08 shape retracted in #168. (2) The exposure is UNAUTHENTICATED, not cross-tenant-authenticated: guest names, phone numbers and purchase data readable by anyone on the web. POPIA-relevant. (3) Filenames are ENUMERABLE — {millisecond-epoch}_receipt.jpg is walkable with cheap HEAD requests over a plausible window; no other secret is needed. functions/receiptTemplateManager.js:386 does the same for receipt templates (lower sensitivity — operator-authored, not guest PII). The real fix is four parts, and only the first is small: (a) drop makePublic(); (b) serve via short-TTL signed URLs minted server-side, or a CF proxy — touches every client surface rendering a receipt image; (c) revoke allUsers on existing objects via a one-off bucket sweep — this is the part that actually closes the CURRENT exposure, and it is independent of the code change, so it can go first; (d) unguessable object names going forward. Recommend two PRs: ACL sweep first (fastest exposure closure), then the code/client change. Found 2026-07-21 while scoping PR-2 receipts.

bugs.title: cleanupOldQueuesScheduled cron has never deployed — export clobbered by module.exports reassignment
bugs.notes: functions/queueAnalytics.js:343 does exports.cleanupOldQueuesScheduled = onSchedule('every 24 hours', …), but line 357's module.exports = { … } REPLACES the whole exports object (CommonJS), dropping that assignment. So require('./queueAnalytics').cleanupOldQueuesScheduled is undefined, functions/index.js:2243 exports undefined, and firebase reports No function matches the filter: cleanupOldQueuesScheduled — it's never been a deployable function. Consequence: automated daily queue pruning is NOT running (only the manual cleanupOldQueues HTTP fn works), compounding the unbounded-node retention bug below. Found 2026-06-01 during the Phase 7 ① ledger deploy (106 deployed vs 107 exports → traced to this phantom). Fix: include cleanupOldQueuesScheduled in the module.exports = {…} object (or move the assignment after it). One-line merge + redeploy.

bugs.title: SEVERITY RAISED to High 2026-07-04
bugs.notes: salesData/forecasts .write root cascade bypass — any authed tenant can OVERWRITE another tenant's EXISTING record, not just create new ones — database.rules.json:495-521. RTDB write-rule cascades are permissive-only: the collection-level .write: "auth != null" already grants access before the child ownership check runs, and there is no .validate reasserting newData.child('userId').val() === auth.uid independent of write path — so the child check is dead code for existing records. Original 2026-05-31 finding (preserved below) only caught the create-new-record case; the 2026-07-04 fresh OWASP scan (docs/security/owasp-audit-2026-07-04.md) traced the full cascade and found the more serious overwrite-existing-record variant (financial data corruption/fabrication across tenants), independently re-confirmed 2026-07-05. Bundle into the same coordinated RTDB-rules PR as whatsapp-numbers/location-whatsapp-mapping (new finding, below) and the existing HIGH-04/HIGH-05/MED-06 write-cascade items — full writer census first (CLAUDE.md step 5e; plan already drafted in PR #166). _Original 2026-05-31 text:_ the H-2 work (PR #96) tightened salesData *reads* to admin + per-child owner, but the root .write remains auth != null. The per-child .validate guards *overwriting* an existing record (owner check), but any authenticated user can still create NEW salesData records under arbitrary keys (no userId-match enforced on create). Fix: add a .validate mirroring the rewards node's pattern — `!data.exists()

bugs.title: rewardsProcessor.js:646 modulo bias in generateFallbackCode
bugs.notes: crypto.randomBytes(6) then byte % 36 over-represents chars 0–3 by ~14% (256 % 36 = 4). Vastly better than the old Math.random(), but not uniform for a security-relevant voucher fallback code. Fix: rejection sampling (while (code.length < 6) { const b = randomBytes(1)[0]; if (b < 252) code += chars[b % 36]; }). ~0.09 extra bytes/code. Surfaced by the #111 auto-review (🟡, on already-merged OWASP-era code).

bugs.title: Recurrence-engine gap: nextDueDate never advances when daily workflow goes uncompleted
bugs.notes: Operator noted on PR #72 preview: "I see one workflow that hasn't been completed daily since it was created." rossSubmitResponse auto-completes the run when all required tasks are done, but does NOT advance the workflow's nextDueDate for the next cycle. A daily workflow created 10 days ago with no run completed reads as daysLate: 10 instead of rolling forward. Fix needs: (a) on run completion, advance nextDueDate by recurrence period (daily/weekly/...); (b) decide policy when a cycle is fully missed (e.g. nextDueDate advances on the next "create run" attempt OR via a scheduled CF that walks every workflow nightly). Touches rossSubmitResponse + possibly rossScheduledReminder (already scheduled). Backlog candidate for a dedicated sprint — recurrence semantics need product input.

bugs.title: Unbounded-node retention policy ticket
bugs.notes: same root cause: in-app "Clear X" buttons are time-bombs without scheduled pruning. Now covers /scanningData (PR #70), /wifiLogins, /activeUsers, /userPreferences, /rateLimitsWifi (all per PR #73), and /billing/debitGuard/{uid}/{requestId} (PR #134 review T-2 — the slice-3 requestId is a fresh UUID per request, so guard nodes never dedupe and accumulate ~1/turn forever; a TTL/expiresAt sweep or 90-day prune closes it), and /ross/agentPending/{uid}/{turnId} (PR #136 review M-3 — abandoned/expired confirm actions are never consumed, so they orphan; a scheduled prune of expiresAt < now − grace closes it — bundle into the slice 7 ross/agent* rules work), and /ross/proactiveLog/{uid}/{YYYY-MM-DD} (PR #158 review — daily nudge dedup markers, ~365 nodes/user/year; rossAgentPrune is the natural home for a 90-day sweep; add before opt-in widens past the founder). Options: (a) scheduled prune CF (onSchedule('every 24 hours')) using the chunked-delete pattern from clearScanningData, prune records older than 30/90 days; (b) TTL-on-write with expiresAt; (c) aggregate-then-discard to hourly/daily rollups. Needs product conversation on retention window per node (different windows likely OK — guest analytics may want 90d, rate-limits only need 1h).

bugs.title: updateWifiDisconnect CF for disconnect-status tracking
bugs.notes: PR #73 made logUserDisconnection a no-op stub because the previous direct RTDB write to /activeUsers/{sessionID} is now blocked by admin-only rules. The disconnect signal is best-effort browser-unload (already unreliable on mobile) so the loss is minor, but admin analytics that filtered by status === 'disconnected' will see all records stuck at active: true indefinitely. Proper fix: small CF that takes a sessionID + anon-UID and updates the status server-side, called from the existing beforeunload handler. Anonymous auth UID must match the record's anonUid to authorize the update.

bugs.title: Walled-garden audit at every deployed Meraki venue
bugs.notes: PR #73's submitWifiLogin CF lives at *.cloudfunctions.net. If a venue's Meraki captive-portal walled garden doesn't include that hostname, the CF call fails silently → fallback to localStorage offline-queue → guest still gets WiFi but no data captured. Audit each venue's walled-garden config (or document the canonical config IT teams should match). PR #73's fallback chain absorbs the failure so it's not user-visible, but it IS silent data loss for any venue with an incomplete whitelist.

bugs.title: Phone normalization to E.164 in submitWifiLogin
bugs.notes: KNOWLEDGE BASE/PHONE_NUMBER_NORMALIZATION_AUDIT.md documents the canonical normalization path. PR #73 stores raw phoneNumber as-received from intlTelInput's getNumber(). Adding libphonenumber server-side would normalize on write. Deferred from PR #73 to avoid the dep + audit-trail effort.

bugs.title: Open-redirect refusal log monitoring
bugs.notes: PR #73 added a host-pattern whitelist on base_grant_url before redirect. Refusals emit console.error("WiFi LOGIN: refused to redirect…") but stay on the client. If a new legitimate Meraki host family surfaces (whitepaper says hosts are dynamic), we won't know until a guest reports being blocked. Fix: forward the error to Cloud Logging via a tiny logRefusedRedirect CF, or include in PR 2's general telemetry layer.

bugs.title: /scanningData .write: auth != null audit
bugs.notes: separate from retention. PR #70 noted the rule is permissive (any authed user can write). The Meraki scanner POST flow writes here continuously; tightening without breaking that flow needs a parallel audit + CF pattern like submitWifiLogin. Defer until after Meraki scanner write path is fully mapped.

bugs.title: PR 2 — Hi-Fi v2 wifi-login rewrite
bugs.notes: closes deferred findings from PR #73 audit: #5 dual activeUsers schemas, #6 ipinfo.io no consent disclosure, #7 weak email/name regex, #8 localStorage PII unencrypted, #11 footer "v2.0" cosmetic. PLUS the automated review surfaced an offline-queue retry-path gap: checkAndUploadOfflineData only fires on captive-portal page load, but post-Meraki-redirect users won't return to that page in the same session — so queued records may be permanently lost for that session. PR 2 spec needs a different retry surface. Flag-gated via customization/useV2WifiLogin.

bugs.title: Double-seed race on signup retry
bugs.notes: rossSeedFirstWorkflow's idempotency pre-check is not transactional. A user double-clicking signup or hitting a network retry could pass the pre-check in both invocations and produce two seeded workflows. Mitigation options: (a) RTDB transaction() on the firstWorkflowSeededAt marker, OR (b) deterministic workflow ID per uid (e.g. seed_{uid} rather than push key) so duplicate write is overwrite. Exposure small; consequence mild (duplicate workflow, no corruption).

bugs.title: Workflow task edit not reflected in restarted run
bugs.notes: operator changed a task's inputType (temperature → photo) via the Phase 4e.1 task editor, hit save, then started a new run on the workflow. The run UI still showed the old inputType. Could be (a) the task editor not actually persisting inputType despite the allowedFields list including it; (b) rossManageTask server-side update not landing; (c) getPlaybookWorkflows caching or stale-read; (d) the operator hit the server's "Run is already completed" 409 case and was viewing the OLD completed run's task snapshot, not a fresh workflow read. Investigation needed before fix.

bugs.title: Task editor UX needs single-Save + success feedback + auto-exit
bugs.notes: operator feedback on the Phase 4e.1 task editor (RossPlaybookWorkflowTasksEditor.vue): currently has a Save button per task row, and saving doesn't exit edit mode or confirm to the user. Desired: one Save button at the bottom that commits all dirty tasks, a success message, then auto-exit back to the workflow card view. Pre-existing scope from PR #32; not a Run-UI bug.

bugs.title: Subscription resolves "enterprise" tier for the operator's test account
bugs.notes: feature-access-control log on PR #65 preview shows Resolved tier ID: enterprise (from: tierId). Tier system is supposed to be free / all-in only (PR #51). The operator's account has a legacy tierId: 'enterprise' value somewhere in subscriptions/{uid}. Unclear whether enterprise is treated as super-tier (all-in equivalent) downstream — likely silently falls through. Fix: audit operator's subscription record + decide whether to backfill to all-in or formalize enterprise as a third tier.

bugs.title: Server auto-completes runs after a single response if workflow has small required-task count
bugs.notes: surfaced indirectly via PR #65 preview. Auto-completion check (functions/ross.js:1311-1314) treats all tasks with required !== false as required. The seeded Daily Opening Checklist has 4 subtasks but none carry an explicit required field — so all 4 are required, and auto-completion only fires when all 4 are responded. NOT actually a bug in the server logic per se, but: the operator hit "Run is already completed" 409, which implies SOMETHING auto-completed the run prematurely. Could be a separate run from an earlier test, OR a workflow with only 1 effective task. Worth a focused repro session before judging.

bugs.title: database.rules.json fails STRICT JSON.parse
bugs.notes: the bookings block (~line 302) embeds literal newlines inside rule-expression strings; Firebase CLI's lenient parser accepts it (deployed as-is for months) but JSON.parse, jq, and most tooling reject the file. Bit the PR #158 verification step (plan's parse check failed on a clean edit). Fix: normalize the multi-line rule strings to single-line (pure formatting, no semantic change), then strict-parse becomes a usable pre-deploy guard — candidate for guard-backfill 3c (a tiny vitest that JSON.parses the rules file).

bugs.title: WhatsApp client PII log scrub (remainder)
bugs.notes: PR #158 masked the recipient phone at whatsappClient.js template-send (the daily unattended path). Remaining unmasked phone logs in the same family: whatsappClient.js:60 (sendWhatsAppMessage, used by booking/queue flows + the sweep's options.fallbackMessage path — currently unused by the sweep) and the OWASP H-10-adjacent inbound logs already partially addressed in #93. One small scrub PR: mask or drop to across utils/whatsappClient.js.
